init.h(3) Library Functions Manual init.h(3)

NAME

init.h - WASM initialization helpers.

SYNOPSIS

Functions


int wasm_parse_args (const char *args_str, char **argv, int max_args, char **out_args_copy)

Detailed Description

WASM initialization helpers.

Provides shared initialization utilities for WASM modules.

Definition in file init.h.

Function Documentation

int wasm_parse_args (const char * args_str, char ** argv, int max_args, char ** out_args_copy)

Parse space-separated args_str into argv[] Sets *out_args_copy to the strdup'd buffer that must be freed by the caller AFTER argv is no longer needed (since argv points into args_copy).

Parameters

args_str Space-separated argument string
argv Output array to store argument pointers (at least max_args elements)
max_args Maximum number of arguments to parse
out_args_copy Output buffer for the strdup'd args_str

Returns

argc (number of arguments parsed), or -1 on allocation failure

Definition at line 24 of file src/web/common/init.c.

24                                                                                            {
25   if (!args_str || !argv || !out_args_copy || max_args < 1) {
26     return -1;
27   }
28 
29   // Duplicate the input string (strtok modifies the string)
30   char *args_copy = strdup(args_str);
31   if (!args_copy) {
32     return -1;
33   }
34 
35   *out_args_copy = args_copy;
36 
37   // Parse space-separated arguments
38   int argc = 0;
39   char *token = strtok(args_copy, " ");
40   while (token != NULL && argc < (max_args - 1)) {
41     argv[argc++] = token;
42     token = strtok(NULL, " ");
43   }
44   argv[argc] = NULL;
45 
46   return argc;
47 }




Referenced by client_init_with_args(), and
    mirror_init_with_args().
